Adler is Austin's second Jewish mayor; spoke at Shalom Austin Generations Campaign groundbreaking (2020); described by former Shalom Austin CEO as "a proud Jew, a friend of Israel." organizations.org: ERCOT / Office of Public Utility Counsel (OPUC)
organizations.role: Residential-consumer representative at ERCOT; chaired ERCOT subcommittees
organizations.notes: Eric Goff, founder/president of Goff Policy, an Austin energy & infrastructure policy advocacy/consulting firm (founded 2019); also serves as Office of Public Utility Counsel residential-consumer representative at ERCOT. Prominent Austin YIMBY/urbanist housing advocate: AURA contributor (26 blog posts since 2019) and described in news as an AURA board member; vocal supporter of the HOME initiative, ADU/backyard-cottage legalization, and zoning reform. Not currently listed on AURA's board page (past/former role). No FEC PAC contributions or personal TX lobbyist registration confirmed.

organizations.org: Eric Winters Goff LLC / Goff Policy
organizations.notes: The well-known Austin YIMBY/urbanist. Eric Goff runs Eric Winters Goff LLC / 'Goff Policy' and is a prominent housing-policy advocate active with AURA (26 authored posts since 2019); frequently quoted on Austin zoning/'missing middle' reform, ADUs, and the housing bond.

organizations.role: Co-founder and Chief Risk Officer (formerly COO)
organizations.notes: Andrew Harris is a co-founder and Chief Risk Officer of inKind, the Austin-based restaurant-capital company (previously EquityEats; provides restaurants funding by pre-purchasing food-and-beverage credit resold via a consumer app), matching the filing's 'Risk officer / InKind.' He was previously inKind's COO and its compliance officer at EquityEats, and before that an employment lawyer at Linklaters, with earlier stints at RBS and a legal fellowship at Immigration Equality; educated at the London School of Economics. He has invested in 30+ restaurants and owned several, including The Guest House and Ember Kitchen in Austin. Caveat: 'Andrew Harris' is a common name, so the 206 aggregate prior FEC donations attributed to this donor record may include other individuals; no ideological PAC giving could be confirmed for this specific person.

organizations.org: Greater Austin Asian Chamber of Commerce

Zip 78746 (West Lake Hills) is consistent with an Austin engineering-firm principal. FEC records list him as CEO, Encotech Engineering, giving to Democratic committees (e.g., $1,000 to Hillary Victory Fund, 9/28/2016), consistent with the 1.0 partisan lean. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

organizations.role: Policy Advisor to Council Member Chito Vela
organizations.notes: Timothy Bray is listed as Policy Advisor in Austin City Council District 4 (CM Chito Vela) and has a City of Austin staff email page; his LinkedIn describes finance, field and data operations on local political campaigns. Donor zip 78751 (Hyde Park/North Loop) sits in/adjacent to District 4, and the self-reported 'Policy Advisor / City of Austin' matches exactly. He is also a published contributor to the Austin urbanist/pro-housing group AURA.

organizations.org: City of Austin Special Events Task Force
organizations.notes: James Russell is the executive director of the Austin Trail of Lights Foundation, the independent nonprofit that has produced the Trail of Lights in partnership with the City of Austin since 2013 after he worked with the city to revive the event following its 2009 cancellation; he previously served as executive director of the RunTex Foundation, which took the event over in 2012. Occupation and employer match the filing exactly. He also holds a City of Austin policy role: he was seated on the Parkland Events Task Force as an Open Space, Environment & Sustainability Committee nominee and served as co-chair of the Special Events Task Force shaping Austin's special-events ordinance. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no itemized federal PAC contributions were found; no Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations found.

organizations.role: Former Director of Research (also Deputy Director, Senior Investigative Researcher) — government transparency and election integrity watchdog
organizations.notes: Kelsey Woerner Speers, MPP, joined Democracy Forward — a Washington, DC progressive legal advocacy organization — in December 2023 as Senior Engagement and Education Advisor, and later served as Interim Director of Oversight & Engagement. She came from American Oversight, where she was Research Director, with more than a decade in government and nonprofit work; Georgetown McCourt MPP and Dartmouth BA. Matches both self-reported employers ('Democracy Forward' and 'DFF', i.e. Democracy Forward Foundation), the advisor title, the DC 20036 address, and a first gift dated the same month she was announced.

Daniel H. Byrne, Austin commercial-litigation trial lawyer, co-founded Fritz Byrne (now Fritz Byrne, PLLC) in 1989; J.D. Ryan Puzycki of Austin (78702) is a writer and housing advocate who publishes 'City of Yes,' a weekly urbanism/housing/public-policy newsletter, and is a fellow of the Roots of Progress Institute — matching the filing's 'Writer / Freelance.' He is a serving City of Austin Zoning and Platting Commissioner (District 7 appointee, secretary; second term May 2025–Feb 2027), a board member of AURA (Austin's grassroots urbanist organization), and sits on the Austin Monitor's Strategic Advisory Council. Career background: equity research analyst on Wall Street, then a decade building and managing Montessori schools. Notable for transparency purposes: he is a sitting city land-use commissioner donating in a council race.

James Russell is the executive director of the Austin Trail of Lights Foundation, the independent nonprofit that has produced the Trail of Lights in partnership with the City of Austin since 2013 after he worked with the city to revive the event following its 2009 cancellation; he previously served as executive director of the RunTex Foundation, which took the event over in 2012. Occupation and employer match the filing exactly. He also holds a City of Austin policy role: he was seated on the Parkland Events Task Force as an Open Space, Environment & Sustainability Committee nominee and served as co-chair of the Special Events Task Force shaping Austin's special-events ordinance. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no itemized federal PAC contributions were found; no Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations found.
